label: Total assets
formula: Everything the bank owns: loans, securities, cash

label: Total deposits
caveat: Doesn't show the mix; see Funding Mix

label: Net loans and leases
stars: Allowance: money set aside for expected loan losses

label: Tier 1 leverage ratio
stars: Tier 1 capital: the highest-quality capital, mostly common stock and retained earnings|Average assets: assets averaged over the period, not one day's snapshot

label: Total risk-based capital ratio
stars: Total capital: the bank's core capital plus the allowance and certain debt that can absorb losses|Risk-weighted assets: assets scaled by regulator-set risk weights; safer assets count for less
caveat: Risk weights are set by regulators, not the bank. Compare with the leverage ratio, which ignores them

label: Noncurrent loans / gross loans
formula: Loans 90+ days past due or on nonaccrual* / Gross loans*
stars: Nonaccrual: loans the bank stopped booking interest on because full collection is doubtful|Gross loans: all loans before subtracting the allowance

label: Loans 30-89 days late / gross loans
formula: Loans 30-89 days past due / Gross loans*
stars: Gross loans: all loans before subtracting the allowance
caveat: The earliest public warning; led the noncurrent ratio by about a quarter in the failed banks we studied (see Compare to Failures)

label: Nonaccrual loans
formula: Loans the bank stopped booking interest on because full collection is doubtful

label: Net charge-offs / loans (year to date)
stars: Charge-off: a loan written off as uncollectible|Recoveries: money collected on a loan already written off|Annualized: a partial-year result scaled to a full-year rate

label: Provision / avg assets (quarter)
stars: Provision: money added to the allowance this quarter|Average assets: assets averaged over the period, not one day's snapshot
caveat: Management picks the timing; usually follows losses rather than leading them

label: Allowance / gross loans
stars: Allowance: money set aside for expected loan losses|Gross loans: all loans before subtracting the allowance
caveat: Covers expected lifetime losses since 2023 (2020 for the largest banks)

label: Allowance coverage of noncurrent (x)
stars: Allowance: money set aside for expected loan losses
caveat: 1x means a reserved dollar for every troubled dollar. Fell below 1x about a year before failure in four of the five failures we studied
warn_label: Allowance falls short of troubled loans

label: Restructured loans / loans
formula: Loans with softened terms / Gross loans*
stars: Gross loans: all loans before subtracting the allowance
caveat: Can hide a bad loan: soften the terms instead of taking the loss

label: Return on assets (year to date)
stars: Average assets: assets averaged over the period, not one day's snapshot|Annualized: a partial-year result scaled to a full-year rate
caveat: About 1% is healthy for a community bank

label: Return on assets (quarter)
stars: Average assets: assets averaged over the period, not one day's snapshot|Annualized: a partial-year result scaled to a full-year rate
caveat: Noisier than the year-to-date figure, but shows a turn sooner

label: Return on equity (year to date)
stars: Annualized: a partial-year result scaled to a full-year rate
caveat: A high number can just mean thin capital; check equity capital too

label: Net interest margin (year to date)
stars: Earning assets: the assets that earn interest, mainly loans and securities

label: Core deposits / total
formula: Checking, savings, and small local time deposits / Total deposits

label: Brokered deposits / total deposits
formula: Deposits bought through brokers / Total deposits
caveat: Leaves for the first better rate elsewhere

label: Uninsured deposits (estimate)
caveat: Self-reported estimate; tends to run first in trouble

label: Volatile liabilities ratio
stars: Volatile liabilities: large CDs, short-term borrowings, and foreign deposits; funding that can leave or reprice fast

label: Federal Home Loan Bank borrowings
formula: Borrowings from the Federal Home Loan Bank*
stars: Federal Home Loan Bank: a government-chartered lender that banks borrow from, pledging assets as collateral

label: Unrealized securities loss / equity
formula: (Market value - Book value*) of securities / Equity
stars: Book value: the balance-sheet value, based on cost, not today's market price|Held-to-maturity: bonds the bank plans to keep until they pay off, so losses on them are not counted until sold
caveat: Losses the bank has not locked in. Part already shows in equity; the part parked in held-to-maturity* bonds does not. This is what sank Silicon Valley Bank in 2023

label: Agricultural loans / loans
formula: Farm and farmland loans / Gross loans*
stars: Gross loans: all loans before subtracting the allowance

label: Construction loans / loans
formula: Construction and development loans / Gross loans*
stars: Gross loans: all loans before subtracting the allowance
caveat: Construction lending drove many of the 2008-2012 bank failures

label: Commercial real estate / loans
formula: Commercial real estate (offices, retail, warehouses) / Gross loans*
stars: Gross loans: all loans before subtracting the allowance
